opportunities.company_name: ExxonMobil vs Utilities Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: Energy sector +4.52% WTD (Finviz), Utilities sector -3.46% WTD — the cleanest sector divergence of the week. XOM up 28% YTD at ~$144 (heygotrade) with oil at $95-100 WTI; Permian/Guyana barrels generate FCF above $60 Brent. Utilities being de-rated as bond yields rise and risk appetite is in Greed zone. Correlation high over 90-day window; spread at recent extreme. Invalidated if oil drops sharply below $88 or rate expectations shift dramatically dovish supporting XLU.
opportunities.trade_setup: sector rotation: energy long / utilities short
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on any intraday energy pullback within $140-$146 for XOM; short XLU at $72-$73 zone.

opportunities.rationale: Within energy sector: CVX is the quality/defensive play (4%+ dividend yield, downstream cushion per heygotrade) while OXY is the highest-beta pure upstream name (Permian-heavy, balance-sheet leverage). In a trending bullish regime with VIX at 17.81, quality outperforms high-beta within sector. CVX fundamentals support relative strength vs OXY if oil stays $90-100 vs spiking above $110 (which would favour OXY). Correlation >0.85 historical. Invalidated if oil spikes above $110.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter after FOMC minutes May 20 if oil holds above $93; confirm CVX relative strength vs XLE.

opportunities.company_name: Technology Select Sector ETF vs Industrials Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: Tech sector benefiting from AI earnings cycle (NVDA reports May 20) while Industrials down -2.76% WTD (Finviz). ISM Manufacturing 52.7 in April is supportive for industrials but does not close the momentum gap with tech. S&P above all MAs in trending bullish regime favours momentum (tech long). NVDA earnings catalyst May 20 is the primary driver — a beat confirms XLK strength. Invalidated if NVDA misses and tech rotates lower post-earnings.
opportunities.trade_setup: tech AI earnings catalyst vs industrial laggard
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter May 19-20 pre-NVDA in $230-234 XLK zone; close short XLI leg if Flash PMI May 21 shows strong beat.

opportunities.company_name: Costco Wholesale vs SPDR S&P Retail ETF
opportunities.rationale: Consumer Defensive sector +3.14% WTD vs Consumer Cyclical sector -2.65% WTD (Finviz) — a 5.8% spread within consumer space. COST is the defensive leader (warehouse model, membership fees provide earnings floor) while XRT contains broad discretionary retail exposed to tariff headwinds. Spread at multi-week high. WMT earnings May 21 could catalyst the long leg if defensive consumer wins. Invalidated if consumer discretionary surprise beats drive XRT above $74.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter May 19-20; watch WMT earnings reaction May 21 for confirmation of defensive consumer strength.

opportunities.company_name: EOG Resources vs Energy Select Sector ETF
opportunities.rationale: EOG is the highest-quality pure E&P name within energy sector: low breakevens, capital discipline, strong free cash flow generation (heygotrade). With oil elevated at $95-100, EOG should outperform the broad XLE ETF which includes integrated majors with downstream compression. LOW conviction — intra-sector pair requires precise spread analysis from live charting tools to confirm z-score >1.5. Invalidated if oil drops below $90 (narrows EOG premium) or XLE rallies on integrated major outperformance.
opportunities.trade_setup: intra-energy quality E&P vs broad ETF
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter only if EOG/XLE ratio confirms widening spread on TradingView; requires manual chart verification pre-entry.
